manipulated_prompt_obj.content: You are a helpful assistant.

Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: australia, goat, watermelon, lion, korea, iran, bear, plum, moose.
Your opponent's team words are: mosquito, hotel, flood, clouds, mansion, wasp, shack, cottage.
Distractor words are: whisk, missile, barbie, spatula, machete, blocks, yoyo.
The kill words are: parakeet.

I'm thinking, what's the strongest connection? "Animals" comes to mind immediately. But then I have to think, "Kill word" - a Parakeet. Could get risky.

So, I'm checking, can I group them by "Mammals"? Goat, lion, bear, and moose all fit perfectly. The opponent and distractor words are all safe bets too (no mammals there), and the kill word (Parakeet) is a bird - not a mammal. This option seems really solid.

But, I need to look at other options. There's also "Countries" (Australia, Korea, Iran), or "Fruits" (watermelon, plum). Each has its pros, but they don't have as many items as "mammals".

Can I broaden it to something like "Wildlife"? I'd get the animals, and maybe Australia since it has a lot of wildlife, but that feels a little fuzzy and risky.

I'm checking if I can come up with some other connections. Like could I link countries to the animals? Australia has some animals. Korea has some animals. Iran has animals.

I keep coming back to "Mammals". It feels the most secure. I've double-checked that it keeps me away from the kill word, and the other words don't fit.

Okay, let's go with **MAMMALS** and target: goat, lion, bear, moose. That feels like the strongest and safest pick!


</thought>CLUE: MAMMALS
